title: Mahalakshmi Temple, Dahanu
tagline: Mahalakshmi Temple, Dahanu — spiritual town of Maharashtra

overview.short: The Mahalakshmi temple, or Mahalaxmi Temple, is a temple situated on the Mumbai–Ahmedabad Highway in Dahanu taluka, Palghar, Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: The Mahalakshmi temple, or Mahalaxmi Temple, is a temple situated on the Mumbai–Ahmedabad Highway in Dahanu taluka, Palghar, Maharashtra, India. It is dedicated to Mahalakshmi. This temple was built by Koli king Jayaba Mukne of Jawhar State in 1306 at the establishment of Jawhar. During the worship, the flag of the present Raja of Mukne Dynasty of Jawhar is offered to the mother's temple.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mahalakshmi Temple, Dahanu?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mahalakshmi Temple, Dahanu is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mahalakshmi Temple, Dahanu?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (95 km) and the nearest railway station is Kalyan Junction (Mumbai) (81 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mahalakshmi Temple, Dahanu?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mahalakshmi Temple, Dahanu at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mahalakshmi Temple, Dahanu?
faq.a: Stays in Mahalakshmi Temple, Dahanu start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mahalakshmi Temple, Dahanu?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vivalvedhe, Sonale, Dahanu, Charoti, Khaniv, Ranshet.
